# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v12.6.0/mod.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
  department: string;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const router = new Router();

// Create user
router.post("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  const user: User = {
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    ...body
  };

  await kv.set(["users", user.id], user);
  ctx.response.body = { id: user.id };
});

// Search users with flexible filters
router.get("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const { age, department, name } = ctx.request.url.searchParams;
  
  let results: User[] = [];
  
  if (age || department || name) {
    // Build dynamic keys based on filters
    const filterKeys: (string | number)[][] = [];
    
    if (age) {
      filterKeys.push(["users", "age", parseInt(age)]);
    }
    
    if (department) {
      filterKeys.push(["users", "department", department]);
    }
    
    if (name) {
      filterKeys.push(["users", "name", name]);
    }
    
    // If no specific filters, scan all users
    if (filterKeys.length === 0) {
      const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
      for await (const { value } of iter) {
        results.push(value);
      }
    } else {
      // Apply filters
      const userSet = new Set<string>();
      
      for (const key of filterKeys) {
        const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: key });
        for await (const { value } of iter) {
          userSet.add(value.id);
        }
      }
      
      // Get full user objects
      for (const userId of userSet) {
        const user = await kv.get<User>(["users", userId]);
        if (user.value) {
          results.push(user.value);
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    // No filters - return all users
    const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
    for await (const { value } of iter) {
      results.push(value);
    }
  }
  
  ctx.response.body = results;
});

// Get specific user
router.get("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const { id } = ctx.params;
  const user = await kv.get<User>(["users", id]);
  
  if (user.value) {
    ctx.response.body = user.value;
  } else {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
  }
});

// Update user
router.put("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const { id } = ctx.params;
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  
  const user = await kv.get<User>(["users", id]);
  
  if (!user.value) {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
    return;
  }
  
  const updatedUser: User = { ...user.value, ...body };
  
  await kv.set(["users", id], updatedUser);
  ctx.response.body = updatedUser;
});

// Delete user
router.delete("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const { id } = ctx.params;
  await kv.delete(["users", id]);
  ctx.response.body = { message: "User deleted" };
});

const app = new Application();
app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

await app.listen({ port: 8000 });
